result:
Subject: Payments outage on April 22 — what happened and what we're doing

Hi there,

Yesterday, April 22, our payments service was degraded for roughly four hours. During that window, some of you saw transactions fail outright — purchases declined, checkouts stalled, retries that went nowhere. That is not the experience you trust us to deliver, and we're sorry. If a failed payment cost you a sale, a customer, or simply your time, that's on us.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that, under a traffic spike, began evicting entries faster than they could be repopulated. Downstream payment calls started timing out, and the failures cascaded. We've corrected the rule, added guardrails around invalidation behavior under load, and verified the fix in production. Full service was restored and has been stable since.

To make sure this does not happen again, we're changing how we ship changes to the payments path: all future updates will go out via canary rollouts with automated rollback on error-rate regressions, and we're introducing chaos testing in staging so failure modes like this surface before they reach you. These are not one-off fixes — they're becoming standard practice for anything touching payments.

If your account was impacted during the outage window, we're applying a goodwill credit automatically — you'll see it on your next statement, no action needed. If you believe you were affected and don't see a credit within 48 hours, please reply to this email and we'll make it right. Thank you for your patience, and again, we're sorry.

— The Payments Team
